Several reports of thunderstorm outflow wind damage in the Bel-Air neighborhood in NE Albuquerque (near Monteclaire and Menaul Blvd NE). Several large (3-6 inch diameter) live tree limbs were broken and snapped. An approximately 5 foot high cinder block wall surrounding a residence was blown over. A residence also sustained roof damage.
Landspout (non-mesocyclonic) tornado touched down briefly southeast of Nara Visa, NM.
several inches of hail and water flowing from nearby pasture across dirt road.
A brief landspout tornado was spotted on the mesa above Petroglyph National Monument. No damage occurred. Exact time and location were estimated. Report relayed via KRQE.

At least one foot of hail accumulation in flooded areas. Via Sedillo washed over with large rocks, mud, and debris in several spots. Culverts and ditches completely overflowed. Water marks indicated at least 6 inches of water flowed over Via Sedillo in some spots.

According to reports, five vehicles were disabled on US 285 and one occupant sustained cuts to her arms. Radar estimated hail size (MESH) was 2.5in over US 285.

Waterfall developed on hillside below Petroglyph National Monument due to heavy rainfall. Video posted by public on social media shared by KRQE. A culdesac near Taylor Ranch was also inundated with hail and heavy rainfall. Time and location estimated.

Right two lanes of I-25 southbound near Caesar Chavez were flooded under 2 feet of water due to heavy rainfall. One vehicle was partially submerged and at least one other vehicle was stalled out. Traffic was backed up nearly 9 miles to Paseo del Norte. Traffic was snarled around the metro for several hours.

Dennis Esquibel from San Miguel County reported a tornado near Sapello. Video evidence confirms. No reports yet of damage.

CORRECTION TO PREVIOUS TORNADO LSR - In coordination with San Miguel Co EM a damage assessment found damage to a roof of one house, overturned chicken coop, and power poles downed between Escondido Lane and Wyatt Dr intersecting county road A1. Roof damage to the home was consistent with EF-0 damage indicator with 85 mph winds. Tornado path length 2 miles and 50 yards wide.

The Las Vegas Animal Care Shelter suffered hail damage to their property, knocking out a window and having crates and kennels damaged. No injuries, two or four legged reported.
